title: Small LLMs, Big Finds: Automated Vulnerability Discovery
modules: Why Small LLMs Are Becoming Preferred Tools for Offensive Security Automation
Code Analysis Pipelines: How LLMs Identify Bug Classes at Scale
Fine-Tuning and Prompt Engineering for Vulnerability-Specific Tasks
Evaluating LLM-Generated Findings: False Positives, Severity Triage, and Chain-of-Thought Auditing
Defensive Inversion: Using the Same Pipelines for Secure Code Review
Building and Hardening LLM-Assisted SAST/DAST Workflows
Governance, Responsible Disclosure, and Legal Boundaries of AI-Driven Scanning
synopsis: Compact, locally-runnable language models are enabling attackers — and defenders — to scan codebases, firmware, and APIs for vulnerabilities at a speed and scale no human team can match. Security engineers who understand how to build, evaluate, and constrain these pipelines will determine whether this capability becomes an advantage or a liability.
rationale: Accessible, fine-tunable small LLMs are democratizing automated exploit discovery, fundamentally shifting the economics of both offensive research and secure development practice.
learning_outcome: Learners can construct an LLM-assisted vulnerability discovery pipeline, critically evaluate its output for accuracy and bias, and repurpose the same tooling defensively within a secure SDLC.
source_signals: Small models also found the vulnerabilities that Mythos found
AI cybersecurity is not proof of work
